8	# =============================================================================

11	# Log levels (from most to least verbose):
12	#   DEBUG (10)    - Internal diagnostics, very verbose
13	#   FULL (15)     - Operational details (file-level sync info)
14	#   INFO (20)     - High-level progress and status updates

26	  tui: INFO
27

114	# =============================================================================
115	# Folder Sync Configuration
116	# =============================================================================
117	# Defines folders to sync via rsync. Each entry is a full mirror (rsync
118	# --delete): everything under `path` is synced EXCEPT the paths matched by
119	# that folder's exclude list. There is no include mechanism ŌĆö to narrow what
120	# is synced, use a deeper `path` or add more excludes.
121	#
122	# Each exclude is an rsync filter pattern, relative to the folder's transfer
123	# root (for /home the root is its contents, so `.ssh/id_*` matches every
124	# user's .ssh). Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom, first match wins. Syntax:
125	#   - No leading / floats: matches at any depth (`.cache/nvidia` matches
126	#     `.cache/nvidia` and `alice/.cache/nvidia`).
127	#   - Leading / anchors to the folder root (`/lost+found`).
128	#   - A pattern with no / matches the final path component only (`nvidia`);
129	#     one containing a / or ** matches the full path.
130	#   - Trailing / restricts to directories (`node_modules/`).
131	#   - `*` matches within a segment, `**` matches across /.
132	# Full syntax (rsync "FILTER RULES"): https://download.samba.org/pub/rsync/rsync.1
133	#
134	# Excluded below: machine-specific keys/config, GPU/shader caches, VS Code
135	# regenerable caches. Users can override by editing this file.
136	#
137	# Always excluded automatically (not listed here, cannot be overridden): pc-switcher's
138	# own runtime files ŌĆö ~/.local/share/pc-switcher/ (state, lock, logs), the uv-tool
139	# install, and the ~/.local/bin/pc-switcher shim (ADR-016).
140	
141	folder_sync:
142	  folders:
143	    - path: /home
144	      enabled: true
145	      excludes:
146	        # Machine-specific SSH private keys ŌĆö never synced between machines
147	        - .ssh/id_*
148	        # SSH known_hosts is machine-local: mirroring it (rsync --delete) overwrites
149	        # the target's copy with the source's, which lacks the source's own host key.
150	        # The target would then lose the source's key and fail the reverse sync
151	        # (sourceŌåÆtarget works, targetŌåÆsource fails host-key verification).
152	        - .ssh/known_hosts
153	        # Tailscale machine-specific config (node key, network identity)
154	        - .config/tailscale
155	        # GPU shader caches (regenerable; GPU-model specific ŌĆö wrong on different hardware)
156	        - .cache/nvidia
157	        - .cache/mesa_shader_cache
158	        - .nv
159	        # Fontconfig cache (regenerable; font paths differ per machine)
160	        - .cache/fontconfig
161	        # VS Code regenerable caches (NOT Code/User ŌĆö that IS synced)
162	        - .config/Code/Cache
163	        - .config/Code/CachedData
164	        - .config/Code/GPUCache
165	        - .config/Code/Code Cache
166	        - .config/Code/CachedExtensionVSIXs
167	
168	    - path: /root
169	      enabled: true
170	      excludes:
171	        # Machine-specific SSH private keys
172	        - .ssh/id_*
173	        # SSH known_hosts is machine-local (see /home note above)
174	        - .ssh/known_hosts
175	        # Tailscale machine-specific config
176	        - .config/tailscale
177

1	# PC-switcher Configuration Schema (JSON Schema draft-07)
2	# This schema defines the structure of ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml
3	#
4	# Reference: CORE-FR-CONFIG-FORMAT, CORE-FR-CONFIG-VALIDATE
5	
6	$schema: "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#"
7	$id: "https://pc-switcher/config-schema.yaml"
8	title: "PC-switcher Configuration"
9	description: "Configuration file schema for pc-switcher sync operations"
10	type: object
11	
12	definitions:
13	  # Reusable threshold pattern for disk space
14	  disk_threshold:
15	    type: string
16	    pattern: "^\\d+(%|GiB|MiB|GB|MB)$"
17	    description: "Disk space threshold as percentage (e.g., '20%') or absolute (e.g., '50GiB')"
18	
19	  # Log level enum
20	  log_level:
21	    type: string
22	    enum: ["DEBUG", "FULL", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"]
23	    description: "Log verbosity level. DEBUG is most verbose, CRITICAL is least."
24	
25	  # Btrfs subvolume name pattern
26	  subvolume_name:
27	    type: string
28	    pattern: "^@"
29	    description: "Btrfs subvolume name (must start with @)"
30	
31	properties:
32	  # Logging configuration (3-setting model)
33	  logging:
34	    type: object
35	    description: "Logging level configuration for the 3-setting model"
36	    properties:
37	      file:
38	        $ref: "#/definitions/log_level"
39	        default: "DEBUG"
40	        description: "Minimum log level for file output"
41	      tui:
42	        $ref: "#/definitions/log_level"
43	        default: "INFO"
44	        description: "Minimum log level for TUI output"
45	      external:
46	        $ref: "#/definitions/log_level"
47	        default: "WARNING"
48	        description: "Minimum log level for external library logs"
49	    additionalProperties: false
50	
51	  # Job enable/disable flags
52	  # NOTE: Only explicitly listed job names are valid. Unknown job names will cause validation to fail.
53	  sync_jobs:
54	    type: object
55	    description: "Enable or disable optional sync jobs. Unknown job names are rejected."
56	    properties:
57	      # Jobs implemented in 001-core:
58	      dummy_success:
59	        type: boolean
60	        default: true
61	        description: "Test job that completes successfully"
62	      dummy_fail:
63	        type: boolean
64	        default: false
65	        description: "Test job that fails at configurable time"
66	      # Jobs implemented in Phase 1:
67	      folder_sync:
68	        type: boolean
69	        default: true
70	        description: "Generic folder sync job (syncs /home and /root by default)"
71	      # Future jobs (add when features 5-10 are implemented):
72	      # user_data:
73	      #   type: boolean
74	      #   default: true
75	      # packages:
76	      #   type: boolean
77	      #   default: true
78	      # docker:
79	      #   type: boolean
80	      #   default: false
81	      # vms:
82	      #   type: boolean
83	      #   default: false
84	      # k3s:
85	      #   type: boolean
86	      #   default: false
87	    additionalProperties: false  # Reject unknown job names (FR edge case)
88	    default: {}
89	
90	  # Disk space monitoring (DiskSpaceMonitorJob)
91	  # NOTE: Config key matches job module name (disk_space_monitor.py -> disk_space_monitor)
92	  disk_space_monitor:
93	    type: object
94	    description: "Disk space monitoring configuration for DiskSpaceMonitorJob"
95	    properties:
96	      preflight_minimum:
97	        $ref: "#/definitions/disk_threshold"
98	        default: "20%"
99	        description: "Minimum free space required before sync starts"
100	      runtime_minimum:
101	        $ref: "#/definitions/disk_threshold"
102	        default: "15%"
103	        description: "Minimum free space required during sync (CRITICAL - abort if below)"
104	      warning_threshold:
105	        $ref: "#/definitions/disk_threshold"
106	        default: "25%"
107	        description: "Free space threshold for warnings during sync"
108	      check_interval:
109	        type: integer
110	        minimum: 5
111	        maximum: 300
112	        default: 30
113	        description: "Seconds between disk space checks during sync"
114	    additionalProperties: false
115	
116	  # Btrfs snapshot configuration
117	  btrfs_snapshots:
118	    type: object
119	    description: "Btrfs snapshot management configuration"
120	    required:
121	      - subvolumes
122	    properties:
123	      subvolumes:
124	        type: array
125	        items:
126	          $ref: "#/definitions/subvolume_name"
127	        minItems: 1
128	        description: "List of btrfs subvolumes to snapshot (must match YOUR system's layout)"
129	        examples:
130	          # These are examples - configure based on your actual subvolumes:
131	          - ["@", "@home"]
132	      keep_recent:
133	        type: integer
134	        minimum: 1
135	        default: 3
136	        description: "Number of recent sync sessions to retain snapshots for"
137	      max_age_days:
138	        type: integer
139	        minimum: 1
140	        description: "Maximum age in days for snapshot retention (optional)"
141	    additionalProperties: false
142	
143	  # Dummy job configuration (testing)
144	  dummy_success:
145	    type: object
146	    description: "Configuration for dummy-success test job"
147	    properties:
148	      source_duration:
149	        type: integer
150	        minimum: 1
151	        default: 20
152	        description: "Seconds to run on source machine"
153	      target_duration:
154	        type: integer
155	        minimum: 1
156	        default: 20
157	        description: "Seconds to run on target machine"
158	    additionalProperties: false
159	
160	  dummy_fail:
161	    type: object
162	    description: "Configuration for dummy-fail test job"
163	    properties:
164	      source_duration:
165	        type: integer
166	        minimum: 2
167	        default: 10
168	        description: "Seconds to run on source machine"
169	      target_duration:
170	        type: integer
171	        minimum: 2
172	        default: 10
173	        description: "Seconds to run on target machine"
174	      fail_at:
175	        type: integer
176	        minimum: 2
177	        default: 12
178	        description: "Elapsed seconds at which to fail"
179	    additionalProperties: false
180	
181	  # Folder sync job configuration (FolderSyncJob)
182	  # NOTE: Config key matches job name ("folder_sync") per naming convention below
183	  folder_sync:
184	    type: object
185	    description: "Configuration for FolderSyncJob (generic folder sync via rsync)"
186	    properties:
187	      folders:
188	        type: array
189	        items:
190	          type: object
191	          properties:
192	            path:
193	              type: string
194	              description: "Absolute path to sync (e.g., /home)"
195	            enabled:
196	              type: boolean
197	              default: true
198	              description: "Whether to sync this folder (default true)"
199	            excludes:
200	              type: array
201	              items:
202	                type: string
203	              default: []
204	              description: "rsync filter patterns to exclude, relative to folder path"
205	          required: [path]
206	          additionalProperties: false
207	        minItems: 1
208	        description: "List of folder entries to sync"
209	    required: [folders]
210	    additionalProperties: false
211	
212	# =============================================================================
213	# DEVELOPER NOTE: Config Key Naming Convention
214	# =============================================================================
215	# All job configuration MUST use a top-level key that exactly matches the
216	# job's module name (the `name` class attribute). This ensures:
217	#   1. Predictable config location for any job
218	#   2. Automatic config routing by orchestrator
219	#   3. Clear correspondence between code and config
220	#
221	# Examples:
222	#   - disk_space_monitor.py (DiskSpaceMonitorJob) -> disk_space_monitor:
223	#   - btrfs_snapshots.py (BtrfsSnapshotJob) -> btrfs_snapshots:
224	#   - dummy_success (DummySuccessJob) -> dummy_success:
225	#   - user_data.py (UserDataJob) -> user_data:
226	#
227	# Future job configs will be added here as top-level keys when features are implemented:
228	# user_data:
229	#   exclude_patterns: [...]
230	#   preserve_timestamps: true
231	# packages:
232	#   sync_ppa: true
233	#   sync_flatpak: true
234	# etc.
235	
236	additionalProperties: false  # Strict schema for current scope; evolves as features are added

1	"""Configuration loading and validation for pc-switcher."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
6	from pathlib import Path
7	from typing import Any
8	
9	import jsonschema
10	import yaml
11	
12	from pcswitcher.models import ConfigError, LogLevel
13	
14	__all__ = [
15	    "BtrfsConfig",
16	    "Configuration",
17	    "ConfigurationError",
18	    "DiskConfig",
19	    "LogConfig",
20	]
21	
22	
23	@dataclass
24	class DiskConfig:
25	    """Disk space monitoring configuration."""
26	
27	    preflight_minimum: str = "20%"  # Percentage or absolute (e.g., "50GiB")
28	    runtime_minimum: str = "15%"
29	    warning_threshold: str = "25%"  # Warning log if below
30	    check_interval: int = 30  # Seconds
31	
32	
33	@dataclass
34	class BtrfsConfig:
35	    """Btrfs snapshot configuration."""
36	
37	    subvolumes: list[str] = field(default_factory=lambda: ["@", "@home"])
38	    keep_recent: int = 3
39	    max_age_days: int | None = None  # None = no age limit
40	
41	
42	@dataclass
43	class LogConfig:
44	    """Configuration for logging levels.
45	
46	    The 3-setting model controls log level floors:
47	    - file: Floor level for file output (JSON lines)
48	    - tui: Floor level for TUI output (Rich console)
49	    - external: Additional floor for non-pcswitcher loggers
50	    """
51	
52	    file: int = 10  # DEBUG
53	    tui: int = 20  # INFO
54	    external: int = 30  # WARNING
55	
56	
57	@dataclass
58	class Configuration:
59	    """Parsed and validated configuration from YAML file."""
60	
61	    logging: LogConfig = field(default_factory=LogConfig)
62	    sync_jobs: dict[str, bool] = field(default_factory=dict)  # job_name -> enabled
63	    disk: DiskConfig = field(default_factory=DiskConfig)
64	    btrfs_snapshots: BtrfsConfig = field(default_factory=BtrfsConfig)
65	    job_configs: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = field(default_factory=dict)
66	
67	    @classmethod
68	    def from_yaml(cls, path: Path) -> Configuration:
69	        """Load and validate configuration from YAML file.
70	
71	        Args:
72	            path: Path to config.yaml
73	
74	        Returns:
75	            Validated Configuration instance
76	
77	        Raises:
78	            ConfigurationError: If YAML is invalid or schema validation fails
79	        """
80	        errors: list[ConfigError] = []
81	
82	        # Step 1: Load YAML with error handling for syntax
83	        try:
84	            with path.open() as f:
85	                data = yaml.safe_load(f)
86	        except FileNotFoundError:
87	            errors.append(
88	                ConfigError(
89	                    job=None,
90	                    path=str(path),
91	                    message=f"Configuration file not found: {path}",
92	                )
93	            )
94	            raise ConfigurationError(errors) from None
95	        except yaml.YAMLError as e:
96	            error_msg = str(e)
97	            # Extract line number if available (problem_mark exists on MarkedYAMLError)
98	            if hasattr(e, "problem_mark") and hasattr(e, "problem"):
99	                # Using hasattr check, so access is safe despite type system not knowing about it
100	                mark = e.problem_mark  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
101	                problem = e.problem  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
102	                if mark is not None and problem is not None:
103	                    error_msg = f"YAML syntax error at line {mark.line + 1}, column {mark.column + 1}: {problem}"
104	            errors.append(
105	                ConfigError(
106	                    job=None,
107	                    path=str(path),
108	                    message=error_msg,
109	                )
110	            )
111	            raise ConfigurationError(errors) from e
112	
113	        # Handle empty file
114	        if data is None:
115	            data = {}
116	
117	        # Step 2: Load schema from package resources
118	        schema = _load_schema()
119	
120	        # Step 3: Validate against schema with jsonschema
121	        validator = jsonschema.Draft7Validator(schema)
122	        for error in validator.iter_errors(data):
123	            path_parts = list(error.absolute_path)
124	            path_str = ".".join(str(p) for p in path_parts) if path_parts else "root"
125	            errors.append(
126	                ConfigError(
127	                    job=None,

content.text: I have a clear picture. Before writing the config, I need your call on a few genuinely high-stakes forks where a silent default could be wrong (secrets, cloud folders, big regenerable data). Everything clearly regenerable/machine-bound (24G `.cache`, 30G `.config/Claude/vm_bundles`, indexers, GPU caches, display config, `.dropbox`/`.koofr` daemon state) I'll exclude regardless.

questions.question: How should secrets be handled? SSH private keys + known_hosts + login keyrings are machine-bound (keyrings are encrypted with your login and won't decrypt on the other machine anyway). GPG keyring (.gnupg) + password-store are your user identity, and pass is useless without its GPG key.

options.label: Sync GPG+pass, exclude the rest
options.description: Exclude .ssh/id_*, .ssh/known_hosts, .local/share/keyrings; SYNC .gnupg + .password-store so pass/gpg work on both machines.

options.label: Exclude all secrets
options.description: Also exclude .gnupg, .password-store, .config/borg. Nothing sensitive ever leaves this machine.

options.label: Sync all secrets
options.description: Include SSH private keys and keyrings too (keyrings likely won't decrypt on the other machine).

questions.question: Dropbox and Koofr are already synced by their own daemons. Their daemon-state dirs (.dropbox, .koofr, *-dist) are machine-specific and will be excluded regardless. What about the content folders Dropbox/ and Koofr/?

options.label: Exclude content too
options.description: Skip Dropbox/ and Koofr/ ŌĆö let their own daemons sync them; avoids double-sync and conflicts.

options.label: Sync content
options.description: Include Dropbox/ and Koofr/ in the pc-switcher sync as well (redundant with their daemons).

questions.question: Your dev/ folder is 56G with ~59 node_modules/.venv/target/.git dirs. Python venvs and some native modules often break when copied to another machine (absolute paths, arch-specific binaries).

options.label: Exclude build artifacts
options.description: Exclude node_modules, .venv/venv, target, __pycache__ under dev/. Smaller/faster; regenerate on target. Keeps git + source.

options.label: Keep everything
options.description: Sync all of dev/ verbatim for a fully seamless switch (larger transfer, venvs may not work on target).

questions.question: Browser/app profiles under .var/app and .config (Firefox 3G, Chrome, Slack, Signal). Their caches are always excluded. Signal Desktop is linked per-device ŌĆö copying its data to another machine won't work cleanly.

# PC-switcher production configuration ŌĆö this machine (nvme0n1p5, btrfs @home)
# Location: ~/.config/pc-switcher/config.yaml
#
# Syncs a single folder: /home/janfr. rsync mirror with --delete, so the target's
# /home/janfr is made identical to this machine's EXCEPT the excludes below.
#
# Exclude philosophy for THIS setup: the point of syncing is to continue work on
# the travel laptop offline (e.g. on a train) without re-downloading tens of GB.
# So big-but-useful regenerable data is KEPT: dev/ in full (node_modules, .venv,
# target/, .git), the uv/npm/pip package caches, and ~/.local/share/uv (the Python
# interpreters those venvs point at). All synced machines are assumed amd64 +
# Ubuntu 24.04 with the same username (janfr), so venvs, native modules, and
# compiled extensions transfer working. Only the following are excluded.

logging:
  file: DEBUG      # keep full detail in log files
  tui: INFO        # concise terminal output during sync
  external: WARNING

sync_jobs:
  dummy_success: false   # test jobs off in production
  dummy_fail: false
  folder_sync: true

disk_space_monitor:
  preflight_minimum: "20%"
  runtime_minimum: "15%"
  warning_threshold: "25%"
  check_interval: 30

btrfs_snapshots:
  # Only /home (subvol @home) is modified by this sync, so that is the one
  # subvolume worth snapshotting for rollback safety. Add "@" if you later
  # sync root-owned paths too.
  subvolumes:
    - "@home"
  keep_recent: 3

folder_sync:
  folders:
    - path: /home/janfr
      enabled: true
      # Excludes are rsync filter patterns relative to /home/janfr. A leading /
      # anchors to the top level of the home dir. pc-switcher's own runtime files
      # (~/.local/share/pc-switcher, its uv-tool install, the ~/.local/bin shim)
      # are excluded automatically and need not be listed (ADR-016).
      excludes:

        # --- Secrets: kept machine-specific ---
        # SSH private keys are per-machine auth identity ŌĆö never copied between machines.
        - /.ssh/id_*
        # GNOME login keyring is encrypted with your login password/TPM; the blob
        # would not decrypt on the other machine, so it is useless there.
        - /.local/share/keyrings
        # NOTE: .ssh/known_hosts IS synced ŌĆö it holds only PUBLIC host keys of servers
        # you connect to (not a secret), and having the same trusted hosts on both
        # laptops is convenient. Only caveat: if the two laptops ever SSH into each
        # other, each will re-prompt once for the other's (differing) host key.
        # NOTE: .gnupg and .password-store ARE synced ŌĆö your GPG identity and the pass
        # store (itself GPG-encrypted) are user identity you want on both machines;
        # pass is useless without its GPG key, so they travel together.

        # --- Machine/hardware-specific: wrong or useless on different hardware ---
        - /.cache/nvidia                 # GPU shader cache ŌĆö tied to this GPU model
        - /.cache/mesa_shader_cache
        - /.cache/mesa_shader_cache_db
        - /.nv                           # NVIDIA compute cache
        - /.cache/fontconfig             # font-path cache ŌĆö rebuilt per machine
        - /.config/monitors.xml          # GNOME display layout ŌĆö this machine's monitors
        - /.config/monitors.xml~
        - /.local/share/kscreen          # KDE screen/output config ŌĆö this machine's displays

        # --- Desktop indexes & thumbnails: machine-local DBs, rebuilt automatically ---
        - /.local/share/baloo            # KDE file index (~838M) ŌĆö rebuilt on target
        - /.cache/tracker3               # GNOME file index
        - /.cache/thumbnails
        - /.cache/gnome-software         # software-center metadata cache
        - /.cache/*.kcache               # KDE Plasma theme/icon caches

        # --- App runtime caches: churn constantly, no offline-work value ---
        # (Dev tooling caches like .cache/uv, .cache/pip, .npm are deliberately NOT here.)
        - /.cache/google-chrome
        - /.cache/spotify
        - /.cache/electron
        - /.cache/IBKR Desktop
        - /.cache/falco-app-updater
        - /.cache/plasmashell
        - /.config/Code/Cache
        - /.config/Code/CachedData
        - /.config/Code/GPUCache
        - /.config/Code/Code Cache
        - /.config/Code/CachedExtensionVSIXs
        - /.config/Claude/Cache
        - /.config/Claude/Code Cache
        - /.config/Claude/GPUCache
        - /.config/Claude/DawnWebGPUCache
        - /.config/Claude/DawnGraphiteCache
        - /.var/app/*/cache              # flatpak per-app caches (Firefox ~1.3G, etc.)

        # --- Large machine-local regenerable, no value on the other machine ---
        - /.config/Claude/vm_bundles     # ~30G Claude Desktop local sandbox VM images ŌĆö re-fetched by the app
        - /.local/share/Trash            # trash can

        # --- Flatpak app binaries: OSTree content store ---
        # Must be provisioned with `flatpak install`, not rsynced (hardlinked,
        # content-addressed; rsync can corrupt/desync it). App DATA under .var/app
        # IS synced, so once the app is installed on the target its settings/state
        # are already there. (A future pc-switcher packages job will install these.)
        - /.local/share/flatpak

        # --- Device-linked app: won't work when copied ---
        - /.var/app/org.signal.Signal    # Signal Desktop is linked per-device; copying its data breaks the link

        # --- Cloud-synced folders: each machine runs its own daemon ---
        # Copying the daemon STATE would make Dropbox/Koofr think the travel laptop
        # is this same install (shared host id / file DB) -> unlink, re-index, or
        # conflicts. Copying the CONTENT folders on top of an independently-running
        # daemon causes double-sync and "conflicted copy" files. So each machine
        # links its own daemon and the content is left to those daemons entirely.
        - /.dropbox                      # daemon state (host id, file DB)
        - /.dropbox-dist                 # Dropbox client binaries
        - /Dropbox                       # content ŌĆö handled by the Dropbox daemon
        - /.koofr                        # daemon state
        - /.koofr-dist                   # Koofr client binaries
        - /Koofr                         # content ŌĆö handled by the Koofr daemon

        # --- Session runtime junk ---
        - /.xsession-errors              # X session log for this boot

## Answers to your questions

**Why exclude `known_hosts`?** I didn't ŌĆö I kept it synced. It holds only *public* host keys of servers you connect to (not a secret), and having the same trusted hosts on both laptops is convenient. The one caveat: if the two laptops ever SSH into *each other*, each re-prompts once for the other's differing host key. Only `.ssh/id_*` (private keys) and the login keyring are excluded.

**Cloud daemon-state / content folders?** Two separate problems:
- **Daemon state** (`.dropbox`, `.koofr`): contains a machine-unique host id + the local file-state DB. Copy it and Dropbox/Koofr think the travel laptop is *this same install* ŌåÆ it unlinks, re-indexes, or produces conflicts. Dropbox explicitly doesn't support cloning its config. So no.
- **Content folders** (`Dropbox/`, `Koofr/`): if each laptop runs its own daemon (the correct setup), both already sync that folder from the cloud independently. pc-switcher then rsync-`--delete`-ing the folder on top races the daemon ŌåÆ "conflicted copy" files and churn. So I let the daemons own their folders entirely and excluded both.

**Why would dev artifacts "break when copied"?** With your constraint (same amd64 + Ubuntu 24.04 + same username `janfr`), they mostly **don't** break ŌĆö my earlier framing was too strong. `node_modules` native modules and `target/` binaries are fine on identical arch/OS; venvs stay valid because the absolute paths inside them (`/home/janfr/...`) are identical, *and* I kept `~/.local/share/uv` so the interpreters they symlink to exist on the target. So per your offline-continuation goal, **dev/ is synced in full** ŌĆö nothing carved out.

## What gets excluded, and why
- **Secrets (your choice):** `.ssh/id_*`, `.local/share/keyrings` (encrypted to your login ŌĆö won't decrypt elsewhere). `.gnupg` + `.password-store` **are** synced.
- **Hardware/machine-specific:** GPU/shader caches (`.cache/nvidia`, mesa, `.nv`), `fontconfig`, `monitors.xml`, `.local/share/kscreen`.
- **Rebuilt indexes/thumbnails:** `baloo`, `tracker3`, `thumbnails`, `gnome-software`, Plasma `*.kcache`.
- **Churny app caches (no offline value):** chrome/spotify/electron/IBKR caches, Code + Claude Electron caches, `.var/app/*/cache`.
- **Big machine-local junk:** `.config/Claude/vm_bundles` (~30G sandbox VMs), `Trash`.
- **`.local/share/flatpak`:** OSTree store ŌĆö install apps with `flatpak`, don't rsync it (app *data* in `.var/app` is still synced).
- **`Signal`:** linked per-device, won't work copied.
- **Cloud folders + daemon state:** Dropbox/Koofr as above.

**Kept for offline work:** `dev/` in full, `.cache/uv` (~21G), `.npm`, `.cache/pip`, `.cache/ms-playwright`, `node-gyp`, `.local/share/uv`.

One thing to note: the very first sync still moves the full retained set (tens of GB incl. the uv cache) ŌĆö do that on LAN/WiFi, not cellular. After that rsync is incremental, so travel syncs only move deltas.

content.text:
Checked both. They're very different:

## Dropbox ŌĆö no
The folder selection lives inside `~/.dropbox/instance1/config.dbx` ŌĆö an encrypted, machine-bound SQLite blob that also *is* the device link (account identity, host keys). There's no standalone "selected folders" file to peel off. On top of that, Dropbox selective sync is per-device by design (stored server-side keyed to each device). So the only way to "sync the selection" is to copy the link identity ŌĆö which is exactly the double-link confusion we're avoiding. Not feasible cleanly.

## Koofr ŌĆö technically possible, but coupled to secrets
The selection *is* in a readable file, `~/.koofr/config.json`, separate from the machine-specific sync **state** (`~/.koofr/syncdb/`, which stays excluded). The relevant part:

```
users.<id>.syncs[0]:
  remotePath: /
  localPath:  /home/janfr/Koofr
  name: Koofr, paused: false, useTrash: true
```

`localPath` uses `/home/janfr`, identical on both machines, so it *would* transplant. But the same file also holds:
- `token` + `oauth2Token` (access/refresh tokens) ŌåÆ **account secrets**. Syncing config.json copies these to the travel laptop ŌĆö against the spirit of your "exclude secrets" choice.
- `syncId` / `mountId` per sync-pair. I could **not verify** whether these are account-wide or per-device registrations; if per-device, both laptops claiming the same one risks the same double-registration mess as Dropbox.

## My recommendation
Leave both as-is (excluded) and set the folder selection **once per machine** ŌĆö it's a few clicks, one-time. Selective sync is meant to differ per device anyway, and you may well *want* the travel laptop to sync a smaller subset to save disk.

If you specifically want Koofr's selection to propagate despite the caveats, the surgical move is: un-exclude **only** `~/.koofr/config.json` (keep `syncdb`, `log`, `recent.json`, and all of `.dropbox` excluded). That carries the folder list ŌĆö and the OAuth tokens, and the unverified syncId risk. I'd only do it after confirming Koofr tolerates the shared syncId; want me to add that single-file exception, or leave it manual?

content.text:
Inspected it ŌĆö now I can answer concretely. It's `vm_bundles/claudevm.bundle/`, 30G, and it's a **Claude Desktop** local-VM (Cowork / local-agent) sandbox ŌĆö **not** used by Claude Code (this CLI, which runs directly on the host). Contents:

| File | Size | What |
|------|------|------|
| `rootfs.img` | 10G | raw disk image of the VM's root fs ŌĆö **active** (mtime today 00:34) |
| `sessiondata.img` | 10G | ext4 "sessions" volume ŌĆö the VM's work/session state ŌĆö **active** |
| `rootfs.vhdx` / `rootfs.qcow2` | 8.7G / 7.3G | the *same* rootfs in Hyper-V and QEMU formats (only one used per platform) |
| `rootfs.*.zst`, `initrd`, `vmlinuz` | ~4G | compressed base images + kernel it re-hydrates from |

So, to your questions:

**Used by Claude?** By Claude *Desktop*, yes ŌĆö to run local-VM sessions on *this* machine. Claude Code CLI doesn't touch it.

**Do we need to sync it?** No. On the travel laptop, Claude Desktop provisions its **own** bundle on first use (re-hydrated from the `.zst` bases / re-downloaded). It's a materialized local cache, not irreplaceable data. The VM is a disposable sandbox OS.

**Volatile / wrong tool?** Exactly right ŌĆö this is the "needs a different mechanism" case, and it's why I excluded it:
- 20G of **raw disk images mutated at the block level while the VM runs** ŌåÆ rsync would move a huge delta every sync.
- Syncing mid-run risks a **torn/corrupt** image copy (inconsistent snapshot of a live disk).
- ~30G because the bundle ships the rootfs in **three formats** for one VM.

Disk images want VM-aware replication (stop the VM, then qcow2/btrfs-send a consistent copy), never live file rsync.

If you ever want VM *sessions* to follow you, that's a separate, VM-aware workflow (shut the VM down, replicate the qcow2), not something to bolt onto the folder sync. Here's what that means for each of your questions.

(The separate `claude-code-vm` / `claude-code` dirs are ~248M each, unrelated.) The origin markers imply the split: `rootfs.img` is a *materialization of a fixed base* (regenerable ŌĆö re-hydrated from `rootfs.img.zst`, which is on disk, or re-downloaded); `sessiondata.img` is the *only* genuinely local, non-derived state (empty origin = not from any base).

Excluding it from pc-switcher can't trigger a download; only *using the feature* does, and that fetch is Claude Desktop's own (~1ŌĆō2G compressed, not 10G ŌĆö the 10G is local expansion), independent of pc-switcher.

I've verified what I can; the rest you must confirm empirically. Here's the honest picture.

## Q1 ŌĆö are the daemons stopped when logged out? Can't confirm from here.
Verified: both run **now** as GNOME-session autostart apps (`app-gnome-dropbox.scope`, `app-gnome-koofr.scope`, launched by `~/.config/autostart/*.desktop`) ŌĆö no system service. Normally those stop when GNOME logs out.

**But** you have `Linger=yes`, and I couldn't pin down `KillUserProcesses` on this box (nothing in config ŌåÆ compiled default, which is systemd-version-dependent). With lingering on, and daemons that fork into the background, survival past logout is *possible*. I won't assert either way without proof.

Verify it yourself ŌĆö this is the one command that settles it. After logging out of GNOME, from an **SSH session or TTY** (the same context you'd run `pc-switcher` from):
```
pgrep -af 'dropbox|storagegui|storagesync'
```
Empty ŌåÆ daemons stopped, safe to touch `~/Dropbox`/`~/Koofr`. Any output ŌåÆ they're still alive; syncing content then = copying a live-written folder (torn) while an active writer races you. Don't.

## Q2 ŌĆö would syncing content confuse the target daemons?
Not *permanently* ŌĆö reconciling out-of-band folder changes is normal for them (you edit files with other apps all the time). On next start they rescan, hash, and compare to the cloud. The issue is what a **blind `rsync --delete` mirror** (which is exactly what pc-switcher `folder_sync` does) can do to that reconciliation:

1. **Silent data loss on divergence.** If the travel laptop has any local `~/Dropbox` edit not yet in the cloud, `--delete`/overwrite destroys it, and the daemon then propagates that deletion to the cloud. Letting the daemon sync instead would have preserved both as a *conflicted copy*. So rsync converts a recoverable conflict into silent loss. (Unlikely in your one-machine-at-a-time flow, but it's the sharp edge.)
2. **Selective-sync fights.** You noted selective sync is per-device. If desk syncs a superset, rsyncing deskŌåÆtravel drops folders travel deliberately excludes; travel's daemon then re-uploads or re-deletes them to honor its own selective-sync ŌåÆ churn.
3. **Internal cache leakage.** `~/Dropbox/.dropbox.cache` **exists** ŌĆö it must be excluded or you mirror Dropbox's private cache.

**The good case (and your real win):** both daemons fully caught up to the cloud before logout + identical selective sync. Then rsync deskŌåÆtravel over LAN makes travel's files already-current; travel's daemon rescans, finds local content hash-matches the cloud ŌåÆ no re-upload, no conflict, **no internet re-download.** That's the LAN-speed / cellular-avoidance payoff you're after ŌĆö legit.

## Recommendation
The benefit is real but conditional; the failure modes (silent loss, selective-sync churn) are nasty and easy to trip. So: **keep it excluded by default.** If you want the LAN pre-seed and will hold the discipline ŌĆö daemons confirmed stopped, both caught up before logout, identical selective sync, always deskŌåÆtravel before editing on travel ŌĆö I can enable it *with guardrails*:
- un-exclude `Dropbox/` and `Koofr/`
- add `/Dropbox/.dropbox.cache` (and any Koofr `.Trash`/metadata) to excludes

On P17, the Dropbox and Koofr daemons stop when the GNOME session logs out. Verified empirically: after logging out and checking from a console TTY, `pgrep -af 'dropbox|storagegui|storagesync'` prints nothing. A TTY login does not restart them (they're GNOME-autostart session scopes), so the empty result is the true logged-out state despite `Linger=yes` on the user.

**Why:** pc-switcher runs its sync while logged out. This confirms the cloud daemons are never live during a sync, so a `rsync --delete` mirror of `~/Dropbox` / `~/Koofr` content could not race a running daemon on the source.

**How to apply:** Both `~/Dropbox`/`~/.dropbox` and `~/Koofr`/`~/.koofr` remain excluded in the personal `config.yaml` by deliberate choice (avoids daemon re-link, selective-sync churn, `.dropbox.cache` leakage, conflicted-copy files on the target where a daemon is live). But the "daemon might be mid-write during sync" objection is now ruled out ŌĆö if a guardrailed LAN pre-seed of that content is ever wanted, the source-side timing is safe.
